name: Slice & Bake Gingersnap Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut four 14" x 12-inch pieces of waxed paper or plastic wrap; set aside.
2. In a bowl, cream shortening and sugar. Beat in eggs and molasses; set aside.
3. In a separate bowl, combine dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, salt, ground ginger, ground cinnamon).
4. Gradually stir the flour mixture into the egg mixture until blended.
5. Divide dough into four pieces. Shape each piece into an 8 to 10 inch roll.
6. Wrap rolls in waxed paper or plastic wrap.
7. Place wrapped rolls in a freezer container with a tight-fitting lid, or wrap airtight in foil.
8. Label and date the container. Store in the freezer for up to 6 months.
9. To make cookies: slightly thaw a roll of dough.
10. Preheat oven to 350°F.
11. Cut dough into 1-3/4 inch thick slices, then cut each slice into fourths.
12. Roll each piece into a ball. Roll each ball in granulated sugar.
13. Place balls on an ungreased baking sheet about 1-1/2 inches apart.
14. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until edges are set and tops are cracked.
15. Remove cookies from the baking sheets and cool on wire racks.
